PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
Off Calendar Feb-10-2021 Off Calendar

Petition For Allowance Of Statutory Compensation To Attorneys
Off Calendar Feb-18-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Kristofer Biorn appeared by zoom for James Harris (Personal Representative).
Philip Spalding appeared by zoom on behalf of himself and the law firm.

The Court noted that Mr. Biorn filed a petition for final distribution. Mr. Biorn stated that his petition has a hearing date of April 21, 2021, that Mr. Spalding has been very helpful in the transition and that he has no objection to an order today granting the statutory fees in the amount requested. The Petition for Approval of Report on Status of Administration, etc. is off calendar. The Petition for Allowance of Statutory Compensation to Attorneys is under submission with the Court's intent to grant the petition.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

Petition For Allowance Of Statutory Compensation To Attorneys
Off Calendar Aug-26-2020 Continued To Dec-30-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Allowance Of Statutory Compensation To Attorneys
Off Calendar Aug-12-2020 Continued To Aug-26-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Scott A. Fraser appearing by Zoom for James M. Harris. Philip Spalding appearing by CourtCall for himself and the law firm.

The Court addressed Mr. Fraser stating that it received his document and that it wasn't a petition. Mr. Fraser stated that he thought that the Court wanted a status report, provided an update stating that the Victory Court Property is to be sold, that the sale of that property will provide liquidity, and that there is a promissory note for $429,000.00 which is one-fifth of the value of the estate. The Court stated that this is a fifteen-year-old estate which should have been distributed a long time ago. Mr. Fraser stated that he understands that they should have filed a petition for continued administration of the estate. The Court stated that it does not anticipate it taking six months to close this estate, that Mr. Fraser needs to get on it, and that this estate needs to be closed. The Court continued the Petition for Allowance of Statutory Compensation to Attorneys to August 26, 2020 at 9;00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Mr. Spalding agreed with the continuance since it is a short set and stated that he will talk to counsel. The Court stated that it expects Mr. Fraser to have a petition for approval of status of administration or petition for final distribution on file by August 26, 2020. The Court further stated that it would then continue the Petition for Allowance of Statutory Compensation to Attorneys from August 26, 2020 to the same hearing date as Mr. Fraser's petition. The Court noted that today's appearance takes care of the order to appear.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Matter not reported.

ORDER OF STATUS HEARING
Off Calendar Aug-12-2020 Text Ruling

Petition For Allowance Of Statutory Compensation To Attorneys
Off Calendar Jul-15-2020 Continued To Aug-12-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Phillip Spalding appeared by telephone for Petitioner.

Mr. Spalding stated that this is an extraordinary 15-year administration, that he is retired and has turned this over to other Counsel, that he has been trying to get this matter resolved for years and that the estate was ready to be closed ten years ago. Mr. Spalding also stated that the Clients took the petition off calendar and that the Executors could not make decisions of getting things done, The Court raised the issue of fees having to be apportioned and questions how it is going to be able to do that on this record. Mr. Spalding stated that when other Counsel took over, they indicated that they were not interested in collecting any statutory fees, that he is only asking for 90% of the statutory fees and that other Counsel can take the balance. The Court noted that it has no evidence of that. Mr. Spalding stated that other Counsel did not waive the fees, that they just advised him, that there is a related trust and that he believes other Counsel is working with the Executors in the capacity as Trustees. The Court noted that it has not heard from anyone since 2011. The Petition for Allowance of Statutory Compensation to Attorneys is continued to August 12, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. An Order to Appear will be issued to the Executors and Attorney Kristofer Biorn to appear on August 12, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to explain why they failed to file a petition for final distribution, or a petition for approval of report on status of administration. The Executors will be removed if they fail to provide a written response by August 5, 2020 with a clear statement regarding status of the estate and why the estate has not been closed.

Judge: Joesph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Cabasares. Not reported.
